verses.text: God raises a destroying wind against Babylon and will sift her, for 'Israel hath not been forsaken... of his God.' His people are not abandoned. Even while chastened, they remain the object of His faithful care.

God raises a destroying wind against Babylon and will sift her, for 'Israel hath not been forsaken... of his God.' His people are not abandoned. Even while chastened, they remain the object of His faithful care.

God raises a destroying wind against Babylon and will sift her, for 'Israel hath not been forsaken... of his God.' His people are not abandoned. Even while chastened, they remain the object of His faithful care.

God raises a destroying wind against Babylon and will sift her, for 'Israel hath not been forsaken... of his God.' His people are not abandoned. Even while chastened, they remain the object of His faithful care.

God raises a destroying wind against Babylon and will sift her, for 'Israel hath not been forsaken... of his God.' His people are not abandoned. Even while chastened, they remain the object of His faithful care.

'Flee out of the midst of Babylon'; she was 'a golden cup' that made the earth drunk, but is suddenly fallen. Her glory was intoxicating. The world's allurements are sweet, but those who linger share the cup's ruin.

'Flee out of the midst of Babylon'; she was 'a golden cup' that made the earth drunk, but is suddenly fallen. Her glory was intoxicating. The world's allurements are sweet, but those who linger share the cup's ruin.

'Flee out of the midst of Babylon'; she was 'a golden cup' that made the earth drunk, but is suddenly fallen. Her glory was intoxicating. The world's allurements are sweet, but those who linger share the cup's ruin.

Men would have healed Babylon, but she is not healed; let each return home, for 'the LORD hath brought forth our righteousness.' Her case is hopeless. What God has appointed for ruin no human cure can save.

Men would have healed Babylon, but she is not healed; let each return home, for 'the LORD hath brought forth our righteousness.' Her case is hopeless. What God has appointed for ruin no human cure can save.

Sharpen the arrows; it is 'the vengeance of the LORD... for his temple,' and the Medes are stirred against her. God repays the wrong done to His house. The desecration of His worship is not forgotten.

Sharpen the arrows; it is 'the vengeance of the LORD... for his temple,' and the Medes are stirred against her. God repays the wrong done to His house. The desecration of His worship is not forgotten.

Sharpen the arrows; it is 'the vengeance of the LORD... for his temple,' and the Medes are stirred against her. God repays the wrong done to His house. The desecration of His worship is not forgotten.

Sharpen the arrows; it is 'the vengeance of the LORD... for his temple,' and the Medes are stirred against her. God repays the wrong done to His house. The desecration of His worship is not forgotten.
He made the earth by His power; idols are vanity, but 'the portion of Jacob' is the Former of all things. Israel's God is no idol. To have the Maker of all as one's portion is wealth beyond measure.
He made the earth by His power; idols are vanity, but 'the portion of Jacob' is the Former of all things. Israel's God is no idol. To have the Maker of all as one's portion is wealth beyond measure.
He made the earth by His power; idols are vanity, but 'the portion of Jacob' is the Former of all things. Israel's God is no idol. To have the Maker of all as one's portion is wealth beyond measure.
He made the earth by His power; idols are vanity, but 'the portion of Jacob' is the Former of all things. Israel's God is no idol. To have the Maker of all as one's portion is wealth beyond measure.
He made the earth by His power; idols are vanity, but 'the portion of Jacob' is the Former of all things. Israel's God is no idol. To have the Maker of all as one's portion is wealth beyond measure.
Babylon was God's 'battle axe' to break the nations, but the tool that served His wrath is now itself to be broken. He uses, then discards. No instrument of judgment can presume on God's favour.
Babylon was God's 'battle axe' to break the nations, but the tool that served His wrath is now itself to be broken. He uses, then discards. No instrument of judgment can presume on God's favour.
Babylon was God's 'battle axe' to break the nations, but the tool that served His wrath is now itself to be broken. He uses, then discards. No instrument of judgment can presume on God's favour.
Babylon was God's 'battle axe' to break the nations, but the tool that served His wrath is now itself to be broken. He uses, then discards. No instrument of judgment can presume on God's favour.
God will repay Babylon for all the evil done in Zion and make her 'a burnt mountain,' useless for ever. Full requital. The proud height that oppressed His people is reduced to ash.
God will repay Babylon for all the evil done in Zion and make her 'a burnt mountain,' useless for ever. Full requital. The proud height that oppressed His people is reduced to ash.
God will repay Babylon for all the evil done in Zion and make her 'a burnt mountain,' useless for ever. Full requital. The proud height that oppressed His people is reduced to ash.
He summons the nations, the kingdoms of Ararat, Minni, and Ashchenaz, against her. The armies gather at His call. God marshals whole peoples to accomplish His purpose.
He summons the nations, the kingdoms of Ararat, Minni, and Ashchenaz, against her. The armies gather at His call. God marshals whole peoples to accomplish His purpose.
The land trembles; Babylon's warriors lose heart and 'forborn to fight'; she is trodden like a threshingfloor. Courage fails. When God withdraws a nation's strength, its mightiest men are unnerved.
The land trembles; Babylon's warriors lose heart and 'forborn to fight'; she is trodden like a threshingfloor. Courage fails. When God withdraws a nation's strength, its mightiest men are unnerved.
The land trembles; Babylon's warriors lose heart and 'forborn to fight'; she is trodden like a threshingfloor. Courage fails. When God withdraws a nation's strength, its mightiest men are unnerved.
The land trembles; Babylon's warriors lose heart and 'forborn to fight'; she is trodden like a threshingfloor. Courage fails. When God withdraws a nation's strength, its mightiest men are unnerved.
The land trembles; Babylon's warriors lose heart and 'forborn to fight'; she is trodden like a threshingfloor. Courage fails. When God withdraws a nation's strength, its mightiest men are unnerved.

Zion cries that Nebuchadnezzar devoured her; God answers, 'I will plead thy cause,' and make Babylon heaps. He takes up His people's quarrel. The wronged who commit their cause to God find Him their advocate.

Zion cries that Nebuchadnezzar devoured her; God answers, 'I will plead thy cause,' and make Babylon heaps. He takes up His people's quarrel. The wronged who commit their cause to God find Him their advocate.

Zion cries that Nebuchadnezzar devoured her; God answers, 'I will plead thy cause,' and make Babylon heaps. He takes up His people's quarrel. The wronged who commit their cause to God find Him their advocate.

Zion cries that Nebuchadnezzar devoured her; God answers, 'I will plead thy cause,' and make Babylon heaps. He takes up His people's quarrel. The wronged who commit their cause to God find Him their advocate.

Babylon roars like lions, but in their heat God will spread their feast and make them drunk, to 'sleep a perpetual sleep.' Revelry ends in death. The careless mirth of the wicked is the eve of their ruin.

Babylon roars like lions, but in their heat God will spread their feast and make them drunk, to 'sleep a perpetual sleep.' Revelry ends in death. The careless mirth of the wicked is the eve of their ruin.

Babylon roars like lions, but in their heat God will spread their feast and make them drunk, to 'sleep a perpetual sleep.' Revelry ends in death. The careless mirth of the wicked is the eve of their ruin.
How is Babylon taken and become 'an astonishment among the nations'! Her cities are desolate. The wonder of the world is now a horror. Earthly glory is fleeting in the hand of God.
How is Babylon taken and become 'an astonishment among the nations'! Her cities are desolate. The wonder of the world is now a horror. Earthly glory is fleeting in the hand of God.
How is Babylon taken and become 'an astonishment among the nations'! Her cities are desolate. The wonder of the world is now a horror. Earthly glory is fleeting in the hand of God.
God will punish Bel and call, 'come out of her, my people'; let not your heart faint at rumours. Separation is commanded. Revelation echoes this: God's people must not share the doomed city's sin.
God will punish Bel and call, 'come out of her, my people'; let not your heart faint at rumours. Separation is commanded. Revelation echoes this: God's people must not share the doomed city's sin.
God will punish Bel and call, 'come out of her, my people'; let not your heart faint at rumours. Separation is commanded. Revelation echoes this: God's people must not share the doomed city's sin.
The days come when He will judge her graven images, and heaven and earth shall sing over Babylon's fall. Creation rejoices. The overthrow of the oppressor is a cause of universal gladness.
The days come when He will judge her graven images, and heaven and earth shall sing over Babylon's fall. Creation rejoices. The overthrow of the oppressor is a cause of universal gladness.
As Babylon made Israel's slain to fall, so shall her own slain fall; though the exiles were ashamed, reproach covered their faces. Just recompense. God repays in kind the cruelty done to His people.
As Babylon made Israel's slain to fall, so shall her own slain fall; though the exiles were ashamed, reproach covered their faces. Just recompense. God repays in kind the cruelty done to His people.
As Babylon made Israel's slain to fall, so shall her own slain fall; though the exiles were ashamed, reproach covered their faces. Just recompense. God repays in kind the cruelty done to His people.
Though Babylon should mount up to heaven and fortify her height, yet spoilers shall come from God. No defence avails. The loftiest pride cannot climb beyond the reach of the Almighty.
Though Babylon should mount up to heaven and fortify her height, yet spoilers shall come from God. No defence avails. The loftiest pride cannot climb beyond the reach of the Almighty.
A cry of destruction comes from Babylon, for 'the LORD God of recompences shall surely requite.' He repays in full. The God who keeps account settles every wrong at last.
A cry of destruction comes from Babylon, for 'the LORD God of recompences shall surely requite.' He repays in full. The God who keeps account settles every wrong at last.
A cry of destruction comes from Babylon, for 'the LORD God of recompences shall surely requite.' He repays in full. The God who keeps account settles every wrong at last.

He will make her princes drunk, and 'the broad walls of Babylon shall be utterly broken'; the peoples have laboured in vain. Their toil is wasted. What is built against God's purpose perishes with it.

He will make her princes drunk, and 'the broad walls of Babylon shall be utterly broken'; the peoples have laboured in vain. Their toil is wasted. What is built against God's purpose perishes with it.
Jeremiah gives Seraiah a scroll of all the evil to come on Babylon, to read it there. The word is sent ahead of the deed. God's sentence is recorded and proclaimed before it falls.
Jeremiah gives Seraiah a scroll of all the evil to come on Babylon, to read it there. The word is sent ahead of the deed. God's sentence is recorded and proclaimed before it falls.
Jeremiah gives Seraiah a scroll of all the evil to come on Babylon, to read it there. The word is sent ahead of the deed. God's sentence is recorded and proclaimed before it falls.
Jeremiah gives Seraiah a scroll of all the evil to come on Babylon, to read it there. The word is sent ahead of the deed. God's sentence is recorded and proclaimed before it falls.

Seraiah is to bind a stone to the scroll and cast it into the Euphrates: 'Thus shall Babylon sink, and shall not rise.' A vivid sign seals the prophecy. As surely as the stone sank, the great city would fall and never recover.

Seraiah is to bind a stone to the scroll and cast it into the Euphrates: 'Thus shall Babylon sink, and shall not rise.' A vivid sign seals the prophecy. As surely as the stone sank, the great city would fall and never recover.
